{'reasoning': "The hemispherical shell is approximated by stacking Lego bricks (32mm x 16mm x 9.6mm) in layers. Four layers are used to build up the shell's thickness (approx. 30mm), with brick centroid Z-coordinates at 4.8mm, 14.4mm, 24.0mm, and 33.6mm. Bricks are oriented with their 9.6mm height along the Z-axis. For each layer, bricks are placed radially and tangentially. The 32mm brick length is oriented tangentially to the local radius, and the 16mm width is oriented radially. The RotationDegrees field represents the tangential angle of the brick in the XY plane. Bricks are placed at positions where their centroid (x, y, z) falls within the defined hemispherical shell volume, i.e., where the distance from the origin is between 80mm (inner radius) and 110mm (outer radius), and Z >= 0. The radial spacing of brick centers is 16mm (brick width), and the angular spacing is calculated dynamically at each radius to prevent tangential overlap, based on the 32mm brick length. This method ensures dense packing to minimize gaps and create a solid structure, while relying on the layer-by-layer and radial/tangential arrangement for structural integrity and support.", 'bricks':
